summary: chai-as-persisted executes remote code from anonymous host at install and require time
details: package.json declares a postinstall script that runs index.js, which loads lib/initializeCaller.js. That file hides a hardcoded URL behind a fake `process.env` shim whose values are base64 strings decoded with atob() at runtime; the decoded URL points to https://amethyst-lorrin-26.tiiny.site/index.json, an anonymous, mutable host. The response body is fed to `new Function.constructor('require', response)(require)`, executing attacker-controlled JavaScript with full Node privileges and the host's `require`. The same code path fires on `require()` of the package, so any consumer importing the module also triggers remote code execution. The package name is a one-edit typosquat of the widely used chai-as-promised, and the package metadata (vulnerability-management description, pino-shaped logger keywords and exports) is inconsistent cover material designed to mislead casual review.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
